name: Kabyle

allophone_rules.notes: Spirantization of /b/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /m/ (e.g., zembil 'basket' вЖТ [z…Щmbil], not *[z…Щmќ≤il]). Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (illustrated there for the Beni Said Riffian dialect; extended here to Kabyle by analogy вАФ see source notes). Corroborated for Kabyle (Chemini) by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /nќ≤/ вЖТ [mb].
Spirantization of /d/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/ (e.g., anda 'where' вЖТ [anda], not *[an√∞a]). Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (Riffian example; extended to Kabyle by analogy).

Spirantization of /t/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/ (e.g., anta 'which one (f.)' вЖТ [anta], not *[anќЄa]; also corpus-attested: ante…£). Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (Riffian example; extended to Kabyle by analogy). Corroborated for Kabyle (Chemini) by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /nќЄ/ вЖТ [nt]. SPEAKER NOTE 2026-07-17: anta is a native Kabyle word вАФ 'which one', feminine of anwa 'which one (m.)'. An earlier revision of this note glossed it as 'you' and a later one dismissed the word as Arabic on that basis; the word is native, only the gloss was wrong.
Spirantization of word-final /t/ is blocked after /l/ (e.g., tamellalt 'egg' вЖТ [ќЄam…Щllalt], not *[ќЄam…ЩllaќЄ]).
Spirantization of /dЋ§/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/.
Spirantization of /d/ is blocked after /l/ (e.g., Ldi 'open' вЖТ [ldi], not [l√∞i]).
Spirantization of /t/ is blocked after /m/ (e.g., tasemmumt вЖТ [tasemmumt], not [tasemmumќЄ]).

Spirantization of /k/ is blocked after any consonant: /k/ вЖТ [√І] only post-vocalically or word-initially (native-speaker judgment, 2026-07). The sonorant cases (after /r, l, n, m/) are corroborated for Chemini Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022, RFP Porto): /n√І/ вЖТ [≈Лk]. Geminate contexts included 2026-07-17: corpus evidence (nettkel, lettkal вАФ k after вЯ®ttвЯ© /tЋР/) showed the singleton-only list let k wrongly spirantize after geminates.

Spirantization of /…°/ is blocked after any consonant EXCEPT /m/: /…°/ вЖТ [ Э] post-vocalically, word-initially, and after /m/ вАФ Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022) document /m Э/ вЖТ [m Э] in Chemini Kabyle, the one environment where the categorical post-consonant blocking (native-speaker judgment, 2026-07) does not apply. Sonorant cases after /r, l, n/ corroborated by the same study. Geminate contexts (including mЋР, by categorical default вАФ the documented exception is singleton /m/ only) included 2026-07-17 for symmetry with the k rule.

Spirantization of the lax velar stop, with palatal shift: /k/ вЖТ [√І] (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.469, Riffian; Karaoui et al. 2024 list [√І] in the Kabyle fricative inventory). Applies post-vocalically and word-initially; blocked after any consonant (KAB_BLOCK_SPIRANT_K_AFTER_CONSONANT). The [ Г] variant reported for Beni Said Riffian (K&S 1997 p.469) does NOT occur in Kabyle (native-speaker judgment, 2026-07) and is not modelled. Geminate вЯ®kkвЯ© = /kЋР/ stays a stop.

Spirantization of the lax (non-geminate) bilabial stop: /b/ вЖТ [ќ≤]. Kossmann & Stroomer (1997), Berber Phonology, p.466 (general Berber statement) and p.469 (Riffian illustration). The tense (geminate) counterpart, written вЯ®bbвЯ© and mapped to /bЋР/, is a distinct phoneme and is NOT targeted by this rule (p.466: 'Spirantization never affects tense consonants').

Spirantization of the lax dental stop: /d/ вЖТ [√∞] (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.466, general Berber; p.469, Riffian illustration). E.g. adrar 'mountain' вЖТ [a√∞rar]. Geminate вЯ®ddвЯ© = /dЋР/ stays a stop.

Spirantization of the lax voiceless dental stop: /t/ вЖТ [ќЄ] (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.466-469). E.g. tafat 'light' вЖТ [ќЄafaќЄ], tawwurt 'door' вЖТ [ќЄawwurќЄ], tameбє≠бє≠ut 'woman' вЖТ [ќЄametЋ§ЋРќЄ]. Geminate вЯ®ttвЯ© = /tЋР/ stays a stop. Applies word-initially before ALL vowels, including /i/ вАФ native-speaker verified 2026-07-17: tifina…£, tilufa, tilelli, tikwal all begin [ќЄi-]; consistent with the 2026-07-16 verification table (tibбЄ•irt, ticcert, tiddi, tigawt). Intervocalic -ti- remains to be verified.

Spirantization of the lax velar stop: /…°/ вЖТ [ Э], post-vocalically and word-initially; blocked after any consonant EXCEPT /m/ (KAB_BLOCK_SPIRANT_G_AFTER_CONSONANT): Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022) document /m Э/ вЖТ [m Э] in Chemini Kabyle. The Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 example argaz вЖТ [ar Эaz] is Riffian-only; in Kabyle, argaz surfaces with a stop [ar…°az]. Geminate вЯ®ggвЯ© = /…°ЋР/ stays a stop in all contexts.
Spirantization of the lax emphatic (pharyngealized) dental stop: /dЋ§/ вЖТ [√∞Ћ§] (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.468). Geminate вЯ®бЄНбЄНвЯ© = /dЋ§ЋР/ stays a stop.

/…£/ is realized as the uvular fricative [ Б] вАФ the Kabyle norm in all positions, including word-finally (e.g., tifina…£ вЖТ [ќЄifin…С Б], bbe…£ 'to dive' вЖТ [bЋР…Щ Б]). Sources: the Kabyle consonant chart (…£ in the uvular column as [ Б]) and the IPA [ Б] reference article's word-final Kabyle example [bЋР…Щ Б]; consistent with the uvular behaviour of /…£/ in the a-backing rules. No word-final devoicing to [ѕЗ] is documented for Kabyle. The broad velar symbol […£] is retained as a documented variant in the allophones table.
Geminate вЯ®…£…£вЯ© = /…£ЋР/ is realized as long uvular [ БЋР]; see KAB_GH_UVULAR_REALIZATION.

Vowel backing: /a/ вЖТ […С] before emphatic and uvular consonants, singleton or geminate (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.463-464: backing/pharyngealization is an automatic, place-conditioned spreading effect, not gemination-sensitive вАФ consistent with KAB_N_VELAR_ASSIM's treatment of velar/uvular geminates below). Fixed in this revision: geminate uvulars/emphatics (ѕЗЋР, …£ЋР, qЋР, tЋ§ЋР, etc.) were previously missing, causing words like axxam 'house' to incorrectly surface as [√¶ѕЗЋР√¶m] instead of […СѕЗЋР…Сm].
Vowel backing: /a/ вЖТ […С] after emphatic and uvular consonants, singleton or geminate (see KAB_A_BACKING).
Default realization of /a/ as [√¶] when not backed by emphatic/uvular context (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.463; Wikipedia).
Vowel lowering: /i/ вЖТ […™] in closed syllables (before consonant clusters).
Vowel lowering: /u/ вЖТ [ К] in closed syllables (before consonant clusters).
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ [≈Л] before a dorsal (velar/uvular) consonant.
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ [m] before a labial consonant.
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ […≤] before a palatal consonant /j/.

notes: Kabyle (Taqbaylit), Northern Berber (Amazigh) language of Kabylia, north-eastern Algeria, ~5-7 million speakers (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997, Berber Phonology, p.461). INPUT CONTRACT: this spec expects text in the standardised Berber LATIN alphabet (the tamazi…£t/INALCO orthography descending from Mammeri's conventions, used by the Kabyle Wikipedia, the press, and Na√ѓt-Zerrad's teaching grammars). Tifinagh (the neo-Tifinagh abjad-alphabet) is a secondary script for Kabyle and is NOT modelled here; a Tifinagh text must be transliterated to the Latin orthography first. Hallmark grapheme->IPA correspondences: c=/ Г/, ƒН=/tЌ° Г/, «І=/dЌ° Т/, j=/ Т/, …£=/…£/, deterministically realised uvular [ Б] (the Kabyle norm, all positions; broad […£] documented as variant), x=/ѕЗ/, бЄ•=/ƒІ/, …Ы=/ Х/, q=/q/, and the emphatic (pharyngealized) series бєЫ бє£ бЄН бє≠ бЇУ = /rЋ§ sЋ§ dЋ§ tЋ§ zЋ§/ (Proto-Berber had only бЄН and бЇУ pharyngealized; the rest are Arabic-integrated вАФ Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.464). ORTHOGRAPHIC NOTE вАФ THE 'v' QUESTION: native Kabyle words are written with вЯ®bвЯ© only; /b/ surfaces as [b] (post-consonantal, e.g. zembil [z…Щmbil]), [bЋР] (geminate, e.g. abba 'father' [√¶bЋР√¶], bbe…£ [bЋР…Щ Б]) or the lax BILABIAL fricative [ќ≤] (e.g. abrid 'path' [√¶ќ≤ri√∞]) вАФ never labiodental [v]. Spellings like вЯ®avridвЯ© mishear the bilabial [ќ≤]; [v] is absent from the native inventory (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997, Kabyle consonant chart). The вЯ®vвЯ©вЖТ[v] and вЯ®pвЯ©вЖТ[p] mappings in this spec serve unassimilated loanwords and foreign names only (extended orthography). Three phonemic vowels /a i u/ with wide context-dependent realisation (a:[a~…С~√¶], i:[e~i], u:[o~u]; Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.463); вЯ®eвЯ© writes the epenthetic schwa […Щ], which is largely predictable from syllable structure / sonority and mostly non-phonemic (ibid. p.463 ¬І23.4.2). THE SIGNATURE FEATURE is SPIRANTIZATION (modelled as allophone_rules): the lax (non-geminate) stops b d t k g бЄН surface as the fricatives [ќ≤ √∞ ќЄ √І  Э √∞Ћ§], while the tense (geminate) stops вАФ written by doubling the letter (bb dd tt kk gg бЄНбЄН) and mapped to the distinct long phonemes /bЋР dЋР tЋР kЋР …°ЋР dЋ§ЋР/ вАФ remain stops and are never spirantized (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.466: 'Spirantization never affects tense consonants'; the lax:tense opposition is durational вАФ lax consonants are 'always realized shorter than their tense counterparts', p.465). Because the geminates are represented as separate long phonemes, the spirantization rules are naturally gemination-conditioned: they target /b d t k …° dЋ§/ but not /bЋР dЋР tЋР kЋР …°ЋР dЋ§ЋР/. e.g. igenni 'sky' вЖТ [i Э…Щnni], adrar 'mountain' вЖТ [a√∞rar]. SOURCING CAVEAT: the worked spirantization examples and the homorganic-nasal blocking rule on pp.468-469 are given in the source specifically for the Beni Said Riffian dialect (¬І23.7.1), not for Kabyle directly вАФ the source's only Kabyle-specific statement is the general remark (¬І23.5.1, p.466) that spirantization 'reaches its culminating points in Riffian and Kabyle.' These rules are extended to Kabyle here by analogy between the two dialects, not from a Kabyle-specific citation. CURRENT REVISION (2026-07-17): (a) REMOVED KAB_SPIRANT_K_ALT and the [ Г] allophone of /k/ вАФ the kвЖТ Г value is given in K&S 1997 p.469 for Beni Said Riffian only; per native-speaker judgment it never occurs in Kabyle. (b) /t/ before /i/: a KAB_BLOCK_SPIRANT_T_BEFORE_I rule was added and then REMOVED on 2026-07-17 after speaker re-verification вАФ word-initial /t/ before /i/ is ALWAYS [ќЄ] (tifina…£, tilufa, tilelli, tikwal); the 2026-07-16 table stands. Intervocalic -ti- (asiti, itiknikanen, tirmitin) is the residual open item. (c) GENERALIZED k/…° blocking to KAB_BLOCK_SPIRANT_K_AFTER_CONSONANT / KAB_BLOCK_SPIRANT_G_AFTER_CONSONANT: k вЖТ [√І] and …° вЖТ [ Э] only post-vocalically and word-initially, stops after any consonant (native-speaker judgment), with the single exception /m Э/ вЖТ [m Э] from Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022). The sonorant cases (k after r/l/n/m; …° after r/l/n) are corroborated by that study (Chemini Kabyle). (d) CORRECTED the BedarвАУQuellecвАУTifrit reference вАФ the study is real but was miscited (wrong author first names, spurious 'BLS 50' venue and eScholarship link, unverifiable 'four varieties/Table 6' claims); the correct reference is Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022), RFP 2022 Porto, HAL hal-03691265. (f) ADDED KAB_GH_UVULAR_REALIZATION (+geminate): /…£/ вЖТ [ Б] as default output, per source check 2026-07-17 (Kabyle consonant chart: …£ = uvular [ Б]; word-final example bbe…£ вЖТ [bЋР…Щ Б]; no final devoicing documented). (e) Fixed broken rule examples (zembil for mb-blocking; snat for nt-blocking), completed the allophones table for all phoneme targets (tЋ§, dЋ§ЋР, tЋ§ЋР and all geminates), and extended KAB_N_VELAR_ASSIM to the geminate uvulars ѕЗЋР/…£ЋР. Earlier revisions added the geminate graphemes (xx, ww, yy, pp, jj, hh), vвЖТ/v/, the vowel-backing rules extended to geminate uvulars/emphatics (axxam вЖТ […СѕЗЋР…Сm]), palatal nasal assimilation /n/вЖТ[…≤], and the homorganic-nasal plus /l/ and /m/ blocking rules for b/d/t/бЄН. PENDING SPEAKER CHECKS (2026-07-17): intervocalic -ti- in asiti, susiti ([t] or [ќЄ]?); sk-clusters of the askar type вАФ spec yields [sk] categorically, but one 2026-07-16 table entry had [as√Іar]; if confirmed without an epenthetic schwa, the k rule needs an /s/ refinement; acki вАФ [√¶ Гki] or [akki]? SPEAKER RULINGS 2026-07-17: tirmitin вЖТ [ќЄ…™rmiќЄin] вАФ BOTH t's spirantize (word-initial and intervocalic), confirming the categorical intervocalic rule in native words; itiknikanen (French loan вЖР techniciens) keeps stop [t] word-internally вАФ a loanword exception the rule engine cannot express (no lexical-exception mechanism), documented here, not modelled; tetwitiбЄН dropped from the verification set (word unknown to the speaker). KNOWN LIMITS (documented, not modelled): (1) morphophonological gemination alternations (geminated …£ surfaces as qq, geminated w as bb, geminated y as gg) are lexical/morphological and are not derivable from the Latin spelling; (2) word-boundary sandhi (n+w вЖТ bb/pp, d+t вЖТ ts) is not modelled; (3) full vowel allophony (iвЖТe, uвЖТo) is only partially covered; (4) Kabyle stress is largely non-distinctive and predictable, and is not marked here; (5) recent loanwords (especially French technical loans such as itiknikanen вЖР techniciens) may escape spirantization and keep their stops вАФ the spec models the native phonology categorically. Labiovelarized k Ј g Ј are usually left unwritten in the standard orthography and are not modelled.

ancestors.notes: Immediate Glottolog subgroup (kaby1244): Afro-Asiatic > Berber > Kabyle-Atlas Berber > Kabyle. Traditionally within Northern Berber.

Arabic adstrate/superstrate: heavy lexical borrowing and the source of most of Kabyle's non-Proto-Berber emphatics and pharyngeals (бЄ• /ƒІ/, …Ы / Х/, бє£ /sЋ§/, бє≠ /tЋ§/), per Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.464.

sources.author: Kossmann, Maarten G. & Stroomer, Harry J.
sources.title: Berber Phonology
sources.publisher: In A. S. Kaye (ed.), Phonologies of Asia and Africa, vol. 1, Eisenbrauns
sources.notes: PRIMARY source, read in full (verified directly, including a re-check of p.469). p.461: Kabyle ~7 million speakers. p.463 ¬І23.4.1: three vowels a i u and their context realisations; ¬І23.4.2 schwa placement by structure/sonority. p.464 ¬І23.4.3.1: pharyngealization described as an automatic spreading feature under the influence of a pharyngealized phoneme; Proto-Berber only бЄН бЇУ pharyngealized. p.465 ¬І23.4.3.1.2: lax vs tense opposition, lax always shorter than tense (geminate). p.466 ¬І23.5.1: general Berber statement that spirantization 'reaches its culminating points in Riffian and Kabyle' and that 'Spirantization never affects tense consonants.' IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the chapter has NO dedicated Kabyle phonology section вАФ only Tashelhit (¬І23.6), Riffian (¬І23.7), and Touareg (¬І23.8) are treated in detail. The worked spirantization examples and values (bвЖТќ≤, dвЖТ√∞, gвЖТ Э/…£, kвЖТ Г/√І) and the homorganic-nasal spirantization-blocking rule (illustrated with mb/nd/nt clusters) all appear on pp.468-469 under ¬І23.7.1, specifically for the Beni Said Riffian dialect, and are extended to Kabyle in this spec by analogy rather than by direct citation. Re-checked p.469 specifically for a claimed /l/,/r/,/n/ blocking rule for /k/,/…°/ spirantization: NOT FOUND вАФ the only blocking condition stated there is the homorganic-nasal rule; the generalized post-consonant blocking rules for k/…° in this spec therefore rest on native-speaker judgment, corroborated for the sonorant cases by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022), not on K&S. Note: the source does not give an explicit, gemination-specific statement about vowel backing before uvulars/emphatics (the geminate-extension of KAB_A_BACKING/KAB_A_BACKING_PRECEDING rests on phonological reasoning from the p.464 spreading-feature description plus internal consistency with KAB_N_VELAR_ASSIM, not a direct textual citation for geminate contexts).

sources.author: Karaoui, Fazia; Djeradi, Amar; Djeradi, Rachida
sources.title: Acoustic Characterization of the Noise Sources for the Kabyle Fricatives Consonants
sources.notes: Read. Confirms the Kabyle fricative inventory [s, f,  Г, v,  Т, z, ƒІ, h,  Х, √І,  Э] (p.123 abstract) вАФ the spirantized velars [√І  Э] are phonemic surface fricatives вАФ and lists spirantization, labio-velarization, palatalization and affrication as Kabyle-specific features (p.123 ¬І1).

sources.author: Bedar, Amazigh; Quellec, Lucie; Tifrit, Ali
sources.title: Post-sonorant occlusivization in Kabyle
sources.publisher: 19th Meeting of the French Phonology Network (RFP 2022), Porto, Portugal, 7-9 June 2022 (conference presentation)
sources.notes: Read. Kabyle-specific study of post-sonorant occlusivization based on Chemini Kabyle (south-east Beja√ѓa), in an Element-Theory framework. Shows lax (non-strident) fricatives occlusivize after sonorants: /nќ≤/вЖТ[mb], /nќЄ,n√∞/вЖТ[nt,nd], /n√І,n Э/вЖТ[≈Лk,≈Лg] (with homorganic nasal assimilation), /lќЄ,l√∞/вЖТ[lt,ld]; crucially /m Э/вЖТ[m Э] вАФ the voiced dorsal does NOT occlusivize after /m/. Corroborates the sonorant subset of the generalized post-consonant k/…° blocking rules in this spec; the generalization itself rests on native-speaker judgment. NOTE: an earlier revision of this spec miscited this study (wrong author first names, 'BLS 50' venue, eScholarship link, 'four varieties/Table 6' claims) вАФ corrected 2026-07-17.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Kabyle language
sources.notes: SECONDARY cross-check (flagged). Used only to confirm the standard 34-letter Berber Latin alphabet grapheme table (c= Г, ƒН=tЌ° Г, «І=dЌ° Т, j= Т, …£, x=ѕЗ, бЄ•=ƒІ, …Ы= Х, q, emphatics бєЫ бє£ бЄН бє≠ бЇУ, вЯ®eвЯ©=schwa, spirantized b d g k t вЖТ ќ≤ √∞  Э √І ќЄ) against the primary phonology sources. NOT used as the source of any phonological claim.

sources.author: Dallet, Jean-Marie
sources.title: Dictionnaire kabyle-fran√Іais: parler des At Mangellat, Alg√©rie
sources.notes: Lexical source for the TTS gold sentences.

sources.author: Na√ѓt-Zerrad, Kamal
sources.title: Grammaire moderne du kabyle / tajerrumt tatrart n teqbaylit
sources.notes: Grammatical/lexical source for the TTS gold sentences.

sources.author: Chaker, Salem
sources.title: Propositions pour la notation usuelle √† base latine du berb√®re
sources.publisher: Centre de Recherche Berb√®re вАУ INALCO, Paris (synth√®se de l'atelier des 24-25 juin 1996)
sources.notes: Direct source for the alphabet assumed by the INPUT CONTRACT of this spec. Explicitly phonological in inspiration: its own consonant chart lists the lax stop/spirant pairs (b/ќ≤, t/ќЄ, d/√∞, k/√І, g/ Э) and prescribes writing tension by letter doubling. This is the 'tamazi…£t/INALCO' notation the spec's grapheme table encodes.

sources.author: Mammeri, Mouloud
sources.title: Pr√©cis de grammaire berb√®re (kabyle)
sources.notes: Codified the Latin orthography for Kabyle on which the INALCO 1996 standard and modern usage build; the conventions assumed by the INPUT CONTRACT descend from this work.

sources.author: Souag, Lameen
sources.title: Kabyle in Arabic Script: A History without Standardisation
sources.publisher: In: Creating Standards, De Gruyter, pp. 273-296. DOI 10.1515/9783110639063-011
sources.notes: Documents that the Latin orthography became dominant for Kabyle only in the second half of the 20th century (Mammeri codification, then INALCO 1996 standardization); supports the script assumptions of the INPUT CONTRACT.

sources.author: Wang, Beini
sources.title: Syllabification and Schwa Epenthesis in Kabyle
sources.publisher: McGill Working Papers in Linguistics 26.1, McGill University
sources.notes: Argues all Kabyle schwas are epenthetic repairs of ill-formed clusters, and that geminates are never broken by epenthesis (e.g. [ќЄaqqraЋРts] 'bottle') вАФ supports the schwa statement in KNOWN LIMITS and bears on sk-type clusters (the askar question). NOTE: an internal project report miscited this paper as 'Yuan Wang, The Kabyle Syllable, University of Delaware' вАФ corrected here.

sources.author: MOKRAOUI, Athmane (boffire)
sources.title: Native-speaker verification sessions for the Kabyle orthography2ipa spec
sources.notes: Item-by-item verification tables of 2026-07-16/17 covering spirantization of /b d t k …°/ in word-initial, post-vocalic and post-consonant positions. Rulings incorporated in this spec: categorical post-consonant blocking for k/…° with the /m Э/ exception; word-initial /t/ вЖТ [ќЄ] before ALL vowels including /i/ (tifina…£, tilufa, tilelli, tikwal); rejection of the [ Г] allophone of /k/; gloss of anta 'which one (f.)'.

sources.author: MOKRAOUI, Athmane (boffire); corpus analysis run 2026-07-17
sources.title: Kabyle corpus audit for cluster attestation and orthographic contamination
sources.notes: 31,085 samples / 21,260 unique words from boffire/tatoeba-en-kab, boffire/nllb_en_kab, boffire/kabyle-english-TM, boffire/kabyle-english-translatewiki. Blocked clusters richly attested (rk 116, lk 100, nk 77, fk 81, tk 103; rg 87, ng 155, zg 63); exotic clusters (xk, qk, zk, …£k) unattested. Motivated the geminate-context extension of the k/…° blocking rules (nettkel, lettkal вАФ k after вЯ®ttвЯ©). Greek/Cyrillic lookalike scan: 25 hits in kabyle-english-translatewiki (to be cleaned at source); other three datasets clean.
